# Code of Conduct

## Our standard

We want this repository to be a respectful, low-drama place to learn, build, and collaborate.

Examples of behavior that help create that environment:

- Being kind, patient, and constructive in issues, pull requests, and review comments
- Assuming good intent while still giving clear technical feedback
- Sharing context, reproduction steps, and evidence instead of escalating conflict
- Respecting different experience levels, communication styles, and backgrounds

Examples of unacceptable behavior:

- Harassment, discrimination, personal attacks, or intimidation
- Sexualized language or imagery
- Publishing private information without permission
- Repeatedly derailing technical discussion after a maintainer has set a boundary
- Deliberately hostile or demeaning language aimed at contributors or users

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to:

- GitHub issues
- Pull requests and review threads
- Discussions, if enabled later
- Other project spaces used to coordinate work on this repository

## Enforcement

Maintainers may remove, edit, or reject comments, issues, pull requests, or other contributions that violate this policy.

Possible responses include:

- A private warning
- A request to change behavior
- Locking or moderating a thread
- Temporary or permanent removal from project spaces

## Reporting

If you experience or witness behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, contact the repository owner privately through GitHub at:

- <https://github.com/SulkBash>

When reporting, please include:

- A link or screenshot if available
- What happened
- When it happened
- Any context that would help review the situation fairly

Reports will be handled as discreetly as possible. Good-faith reporters should not face retaliation for raising a concern.
